pr.title: Split plstale into a new "plattack" and match some of the functions, setup appropriate function prototypes
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Separated the latter 0x80037590-0x80037B2C portion of plstale into a new plattack unit, renamed the affected symbols from generic pl_* names to plStale_* and plAttack_*, added initial C matches for small stale/attack counter helpers, introduced a StaleMoveTable struct, and updated build/split/symbol metadata plus callsites across fighter, item, and player code.
what_changed: Split the original plstale text range in config/GALE01/splits.txt: plstale.c now covers 0x8003715C-0x80037590 and new plattack.c covers 0x80037590-0x80037B2C.
Moved the corresponding assembly from asm/melee/pl/plstale.s into a new asm/melee/pl/plattack.s; plstale.s deleted roughly the moved attack-counter portion.
Renamed symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from pl_8003715C/pl_80037168/pl_80037208/pl_8003722C/pl_800373CC to plStale_* and from pl_80037590/pl_8003759C/pl_80037B08 to plAttack_*.
Updated assembly callsites in fighter, collision, item, and bonus code to branch to plStale_* or plAttack_* rather than the old generic pl_* names.
Added C implementations for plStale_8003715C, plStale_80037168, and plStale_80037208 in src/melee/pl/plstale.c.
Added C implementations for plAttack_80037590 and plAttack_80037B08 in src/melee/pl/plattack.c.
Added src/melee/pl/plstale.h and src/melee/pl/plattack.h, and removed stale-related prototypes from the broad placeholder header src/melee/pl/pl_0371.h.
Introduced StaleMoveTable in src/melee/pl/types.h and replaced individual StaticPlayer stale_move_table_current_write_index and stale_move_index_0..9 fields with a single stale_moves member.
Changed Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr and Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr2 to return StaleMoveTable* instead of s32*, returning &player->stale_moves.
Updated Player_InitAllPlayers to call plStale_8003715C and plAttack_80037590.
Added plattack.c to configure.py as a NonMatching object and added asm/melee/pl/plattack.s.o to obj_files.mk.
Adjusted an archive local in Player_80036DD8 from int* to void**, likely to match lbArchive_80016C64 usage.
smart_moves: The PR split the code at a clean address boundary and mirrored that split in splits.txt, symbols.txt, assembly files, source files, and object lists, avoiding a half-renamed module state.
Renaming by subsystem intent, plStale_* versus plAttack_*, made callsites more searchable and reduced ambiguity from the old pl_* prefix.

Extracting StaleMoveTable converted a run of manually named stale_move_index fields into a typed aggregate with documented current_index and 10 entries, making the matched reset loop in plStale_80037168 straightforward.
The small 16-bit counter helpers were good early matching targets: both stale and attack counters initialize to 1, increment as u16 values, skip zero on wraparound, and return the previous value.
Moving prototypes out of pl_0371.h into focused plstale/plattack headers reduced dependence on placeholder declarations and gave dependent files a clearer include path.
The build configuration was updated in both configure.py and obj_files.mk, which is important when a source split has both nonmatching C and assembly object implications.
decomp_lessons: When a contiguous assembly blob actually contains different logical systems, split it only after updating split ranges, symbol names, object lists, assembly filenames, and all cross-module callsites together.
Use address-preserving subsystem prefixes such as plStale_80037208 and plAttack_80037B08 when semantics are known but final descriptive names are not yet available.
Replacing offset-based field runs with a small struct can make reset/clear loops expressible in C while preserving layout; here StaticPlayer offset 0xBC became StaleMoveTable stale_moves.
Counter helpers that avoid returning or storing zero after u16 wrap can often be matched with a local `before` variable, increment, zero check, and return-before pattern.
Do not leave old prototypes in catchall placeholder headers after focused headers exist; stale declarations can hide type mismatches during later decomp work.
For module splits, remember that sbss ownership may need to move too: this PR assigned player.c 0x804D6470-0x804D6478, plstale.c 0x804D6478-0x804D6480, and plattack.c 0x804D6480-0x804D6488.
naming_conventions: Use camel-style subsystem prefixes with retained addresses: plStale_8003715C, plStale_80037208, plAttack_80037590, plAttack_80037B08.
New file/module names follow lowercase subsystem names: plstale.c/plstale.h and plattack.c/plattack.h.
Typed player table name used in StaticPlayer is `stale_moves`; the struct type is `StaleMoveTable`.
The old broad pl_0371.h prototypes were removed once plstale.h and plattack.h were introduced.

Evidence caveat: the diff excerpt for plattack.h shows `u16 plStale_80037B08(void);` while plattack.c and symbols.txt use `plAttack_80037B08`; future work should verify header prototypes exactly match definitions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved branch targets in assembly while renaming callee symbols, e.g. calls from ft_0881.s and it_2725.s to pl_80037208 became plStale_80037208, and calls to pl_80037B08 became plAttack_80037B08.
Matched plStale_80037208 and plAttack_80037B08 with the same u16 increment/wrap-skip-zero idiom: store `before`, increment global, if the global becomes zero set it to one, then return `before`.
Matched plStale_80037168 by using Player_GetStaleMoveTableIndexPtr(slot), setting current_index to zero, and looping over 10 stale move entries to clear move_id and num_action_states.
Kept larger plAttack_8003759C in assembly/nonmatching while extracting smaller confirmed functions first, limiting matching risk.
Updated .sbss split boundaries alongside text split boundaries so globals like unk_804D6478 and unk_804D6480 belong to the intended module.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Suggested the VSCode EditorConfig extension: "This thing might make your life easier assuming you're using VSCode."
review_feedback.lesson: No substantive code-review feedback was captured in the slice; the only human comment was an editor/tooling suggestion.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename plStale_* or plAttack_* symbols to final descriptive names.
Find later fixes to src/melee/pl/plattack.h to confirm whether `plStale_80037B08` was a header typo in this slice.
Search for further StaleMoveTable layout refinements or field renames after PR 1164.
Search for PRs that match plAttack_8003759C, the large attack table initialization/reset routine left nonmatching here.
Check whether unk_804D6478 and unk_804D6480 later receive semantic names.
